title: Simplest DIY Bed Frame (All Mattress Sizes)
summary: A 2x6 box frame with 2x2 cleats and screwed-down 1x4 slats — no headboard, no footboard, just the minimum structure a mattress needs, in five real sizes.
description:
This is the bed frame stripped to its studs: a 2x6 perimeter box, 2x2 cleats to carry the slats, and a center 2x4 rail so a queen or king doesn't sag in the middle. There's no headboard or footboard here at all — pair it with one you already own, lean it against a wall, or add a simple panel of your own later, since the frame itself doesn't depend on either.

The plan gives five complete, genuinely distinct cut lists — twin through California king — not one size scaled by guesswork, so build to the numbers for your actual mattress rather than interpolating between two sizes. King-size beds need the additional center leg supports in the last step; smaller sizes can skip them. The corner bracing is optional but adds real rigidity for very little extra material, and it's a good place to spend an extra 20 minutes if the bed will see rough use.

materials.name: Pine, 2x6, 8 ft
materials.note: Sized for twin — add boards per the size-specific shopping list for larger mattresses.

materials.name: Pine, 1x4, 8 ft
materials.note: Slats — scrap lumber works as long as the thickness is consistent.

cutList.note: Twin length shown — see the size-specific note for other mattress sizes.
Twin width shown; 57"/63"/79"/75" for full/queen/king/cal-king respectively.
Half slats can be used if working from scrap.

steps.title: Attach the cleats to the side rails
steps.body: On the inside face of both side rails, attach the 2x2 cleats with 2-1/2" screws.

steps.title: Assemble the box
steps.body: Attach the top and bottom rails to the side rails to complete the perimeter box.

steps.title: Add the center support
steps.body: For full size and up, add the center 2x4 rail so its top surface sits flush with the top of the 2x2 cleats — this is what keeps a wide mattress from sagging in the middle over the unsupported span.

steps.title: Add optional corner bracing
steps.body: For extra strength, add corner braces cut from 2x6 with both ends at 45°, screwed in from both sides.

steps.title: Lay and attach the slats
steps.body: Lay the slats across the 2x2 cleats and the center 2x4, then screw each one down. Half slats work fine if you're using up scrap material.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Attach the bed legs to the underside of the frame with screws.

steps.title: Add center leg support for king sizes
steps.body: For standard king and California king, add the center leg supports called out in the cut list to help carry the extra weight across the wider center span.

images.alt: A minimal 2x6 box bed frame with slats and legs, no headboard or footboard
